heading: View Key Used to Encrypt a Snapshot
body:
For clusters using Azure Key Vault
as their Key Management Service, Atlas uses the project's
Key Identifier, Key Vault Credentials, and Active Directory
application account credentials at the time of the snapshot to
automatically encrypt the snapshot data files. This is an
additional layer of encryption on the existing encryption
applied to all Atlas storage and snapshot volumes.
[oplog](manual/reference/glossary) data collected for PIT (Point in Time) restores
is also encrypted with the customer's CMK (customer-managed key).

Atlas stores the unique ID of the Azure Key Identifier used
the encrypt the snapshot. Atlas also stores the Azure Key
Vault credentials and the Active Domain application account
credentials used to access the Key Identifier. Atlas uses
this information when restoring the snapshot. You can
[Access an Encrypted Snapshot](access-encrypted-snapshot) and
[restore a snapshot using Encryption at Rest](restore-from-ear).
